title: Simple Synthetic Angiography 0.2
author: Batten, James
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This 2D synthetic dataset is composed of 15,248 train and 3,812 test trees. This dataset is intended to both facilitate reproducibility and support development of data-driven tree extraction algorithms. 
 The dataset is generated in two steps: the first step produces the tree geometry, and the second renders the corresponding image. The first step models the angiogenesis process using a simple force-based simulation which iteratively grows a tree composed of a set of nodes and edges. Murray's law is used to assign the vessel diameter to each location along the centerline. Note that the SSA dataset does not contain trifurcations. This revision of the dataset adds the missing radius term, making the centerline data consistent with the segmentation mask. 
 More information here:  https://github.com/JamesBatten/SimpleSyntheticAngiography
steps_raw:
This 2D synthetic dataset is composed of 15,248 train and 3,812 test trees. This dataset is intended to both facilitate reproducibility and support development of data-driven tree extraction algorithms. 
 The dataset is generated in two steps: the first step produces the tree geometry, and the second renders the corresponding image. The first step models the angiogenesis process using a simple force-based simulation which iteratively grows a tree composed of a set of nodes and edges. Murray's law is used to assign the vessel diameter to each location along the centerline. Note that the SSA dataset does not contain trifurcations. This revision of the dataset adds the missing radius term, making the centerline data consistent with the segmentation mask. 
 More information here:  https://github.com/JamesBatten/SimpleSyntheticAngiography
